---

## What is Ceezix?

Ceezix is a modular AI-driven development platform ‚ÄĒ not a single app, but a growing ecosystem of intelligent systems built for developers who move fast and build differently.

It is engineered around three principles:

- **Automation first** ‚ÄĒ every repetitive workflow is a candidate for elimination
- **Modular by design** ‚ÄĒ each system is independently deployable, composable at scale
- **Intelligence at the core** ‚ÄĒ AI is not a feature, it is the foundation

Built inside a real Termux / mobile dev environment. Every component is tested under actual constraints ‚ÄĒ no sandboxed ideal conditions.

---

## Architecture

```
ceezix/
‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ apps/
‚Ēā   ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ api/          ‚Üí Express backend ‚ÄĒ AI services, automation logic, REST APIs
‚Ēā   ‚ĒĒ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ web/          ‚Üí React (Vite) ‚ÄĒ developer-facing interface and dashboards
‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ packages/
‚Ēā   ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ core/         ‚Üí Shared logic, utilities, AI abstractions
‚Ēā   ‚ĒĒ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ config/       ‚Üí Unified configuration layer
‚ĒĒ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ tools/            ‚Üí CLI utilities, build scripts, network tooling
```

A monorepo structure managed with PNPM workspaces. Each `apps/` module runs independently. Shared logic lives in `packages/` and is consumed without duplication.

---

## Core Capabilities

| Domain | Description |
|---|---|
| **AI Systems** | Inference pipelines, prompt engineering layers, model integrations |
| **Automation** | Workflow engines, scheduled task runners, event-driven triggers |
| **API Layer** | RESTful services with structured response contracts and auth |
| **Dev Tooling** | CLI utilities, scaffolding tools, environment configuration |
| **Network Optimization** | Custom scripting layer for bandwidth, routing, and performance |

---

## Featured Modules

<table>
<tr>
<td width="50%">

### ‚ö° API Engine
`ceezix-api`

The backend core. Handles all AI service routing, automation job execution, and external integrations. Built on Express with a structured middleware pipeline.

</td>
<td width="50%">

### ūüĖ•ÔłŹ UI Dashboard
`ceezix-web`

React interface for monitoring, configuration, and interacting with Ceezix systems. Built with Vite for instant feedback loops during development.

</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td width="50%">

### ūüĆź Network Engine
`ceezix-net`

Low-level network tooling and optimization scripts. DNS, bandwidth management, connection profiling ‚ÄĒ built for real environments, not ideal ones.

</td>
<td width="50%">

### ūüß† AI Core
`ceezix-core`

The intelligence layer. Abstractions over AI models, prompt lifecycle management, response parsing, and context handling across all Ceezix modules.

</td>
</tr>
</table>

---

## Tech Stack

```
Runtime       ‚Üí  Node.js
Backend       ‚Üí  Express.js
Frontend      ‚Üí  React + Vite
Package Mgr   ‚Üí  PNPM (workspaces)
Language      ‚Üí  JavaScript (ES Modules)
Environment   ‚Üí  Termux ¬∑ Linux ¬∑ Mobile-native
```

---

## Live System Mindset

Ceezix is built in production-equivalent conditions from day one.

- **Real environment** ‚ÄĒ developed on Termux, tested on constrained hardware
- **Performance-optimized** ‚ÄĒ no bloat, no unnecessary dependencies
- **Horizontally scalable** ‚ÄĒ modules can be extracted, replicated, or replaced
- **Zero tolerance for technical debt** ‚ÄĒ architecture decisions are made to last

This is not a prototype. It is a platform.

---

## Vision

```
2025  ‚Üí  Core platform (API + Web + AI layer)
2026  ‚Üí  Automation ecosystem, CLI suite, network tooling
2027  ‚Üí  Mobile overlays, AI terminal OS, distributed agent framework
```

The end goal: a fully autonomous developer environment where Ceezix handles orchestration, optimization, and execution ‚ÄĒ so the engineer focuses on architecture, not operations.

## Organization Structure

| Repository | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `ceezix-api` | Express backend, AI services, automation engine |
| `ceezix-web` | React/Vite frontend dashboard |
| `ceezix-core` | Shared packages, AI abstractions, utilities |
| `ceezix-net` | Network tooling and optimization scripts |
| `ceezix-cli` | Command-line interface and developer tools |
| `.github` | Organization profile and community standards |
